findings.issue: The action s3:GetObject is scoped to an IAM role ARN (arn:aws:iam::533335673158:role/SomeRole). S3 actions only apply to S3 bucket/object ARNs (arn:aws:s3:::...), so this permission will never actually grant access to anything.
findings.recommendation: Change the Resource to a valid S3 object ARN, e.g., arn:aws:s3:::my-bucket/* or arn:aws:s3:::my-bucket/prefix/*.

findings.issue: The statement lacks a Sid, making it harder to identify and audit in larger policies.
findings.recommendation: Add a descriptive Sid (e.g., "Sid": "AllowS3GetObject") to improve readability and auditability.

summary: The policy is effectively a no-op because the S3 action is paired with an IAM role ARN that will never match any S3 request.
